---
title: "Data Analysis"
format: pdf
---

```{r}
library(dplyr)
student_data <- read.csv("C:/fall 2025/stat 6750/StudentPerformanceFactors.csv")
head(student_data)
colSums(is.na(student_data))
```

```{r}
student_data_clean <- student_data[!apply(student_data == "", 1, any), ]

# Check the dimensions before and after
cat("Original rows:", nrow(student_data), "\n")
cat("After removing empty strings:", nrow(student_data_clean), "\n")
cat("Rows removed:", nrow(student_data) - nrow(student_data_clean), "\n")

# Verify no empty strings remain
sapply(student_data_clean, function(x) sum(x == "", na.rm = TRUE))


# WE REMOVE THE ROWS THAT HAVE EMPTY STRING ""  
```

```{r}

# Remove the low-variance and unwanted columns
student_data_filtered <- student_data_clean %>%
  select(-Internet_Access, -Learning_Disabilities, -Gender)

# WE REMOVE THE ABOVE MENTIONED ROWS COZ THEY HAVE LESS VARIABILITY 

# Check what we have left
cat("Remaining variables:\n")
print(colnames(student_data_filtered))

cat("\nNumber of variables:", ncol(student_data_filtered), "\n")
cat("Number of observations:", nrow(student_data_filtered), "\n")

# Check the structure
str(student_data_filtered) 


# 1. UNIVARIATE ANALYSIS - Each variable vs Exam_Score


cat("=== CORRELATION ANALYSIS (Numeric Variables) ===\n")
numeric_vars <- c("Hours_Studied", "Attendance", "Sleep_Hours", 
                  "Previous_Scores", "Tutoring_Sessions", "Physical_Activity")

for(var in numeric_vars) {
  cor_val <- cor(student_data_filtered[[var]], student_data_filtered$Exam_Score)
  cat(var, ": ", round(cor_val, 4), "\n")
}

# WE REMOVE SLEEP_HOURS LESS CORRELATION AND PHYSICAL_ACTIVITY (PHYSICAL_ACTIVITY NOT DEFINED PROPERLY IN THE DATASET)
```

```{r}

# 2. ANOVA F-TEST (Categorical Variables)


cat("\n=== ANOVA F-TEST (Categorical Variables) ===\n")
cat("Higher F-statistic = stronger relationship with Exam_Score\n\n")

categorical_vars <- c("Parental_Involvement", "Access_to_Resources", 
                      "Extracurricular_Activities", "Motivation_Level",
                      "Family_Income", "Teacher_Quality", "School_Type",
                      "Peer_Influence", "Parental_Education_Level", 
                      "Distance_from_Home")

anova_results <- data.frame()

for(var in categorical_vars) {
  formula <- as.formula(paste("Exam_Score ~", var))
  anova_model <- aov(formula, data = student_data_filtered)
  anova_summary <- summary(anova_model)
  
  f_stat <- anova_summary[[1]]$`F value`[1]
  p_value <- anova_summary[[1]]$`Pr(>F)`[1]
  
  cat(sprintf("%-30s F-stat: %8.2f   p-value: %.4f\n", var, f_stat, p_value))
  
  anova_results <- rbind(anova_results, 
                         data.frame(Variable = var, F_stat = f_stat, p_value = p_value))
}

cat("\n=== VARIABLES RANKED BY F-STATISTIC ===\n")
anova_results <- anova_results[order(-anova_results$F_stat), ]
print(anova_results)
# WE REMOVE SCHOOL_TYPE BECAUSE OF A LOW F STATISTIC 
```

```{r}

# MEAN DIFFERENCE ANALYSIS


cat("=== PRACTICAL SIGNIFICANCE - Mean Score Differences ===\n\n")

categorical_vars <- c("Parental_Involvement", "Access_to_Resources", 
                      "Extracurricular_Activities", "Motivation_Level",
                      "Family_Income", "Teacher_Quality",
                      "Peer_Influence", "Parental_Education_Level", 
                      "Distance_from_Home")

for(var in categorical_vars) {
  cat("\n", var, ":\n")
  cat(strrep("=", 50), "\n")
  
  result <- student_data_filtered %>%
    group_by(across(all_of(var))) %>%
    summarise(
      Mean_Score = round(mean(Exam_Score), 2),
      Median_Score = round(median(Exam_Score), 2),
      SD = round(sd(Exam_Score), 2),
      Count = n(),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    arrange(desc(Mean_Score))
  
  max_diff <- max(result$Mean_Score) - min(result$Mean_Score)
  cat("Maximum difference:", round(max_diff, 2), "points\n")
  cat("(Difference between best and worst category)\n\n")
  print(result)
}

# Summary table of all differences
cat("\n\n=== SUMMARY: ALL VARIABLES RANKED BY PRACTICAL IMPACT ===\n")
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n\n")

summary_df <- data.frame()

for(var in categorical_vars) {
  result <- student_data_filtered %>%
    group_by(across(all_of(var))) %>%
    summarise(Mean_Score = mean(Exam_Score), .groups = "drop")
  
  max_diff <- max(result$Mean_Score) - min(result$Mean_Score)
  
  summary_df <- rbind(summary_df, 
                      data.frame(Variable = var, 
                                Max_Difference = round(max_diff, 2)))
}

summary_df <- summary_df[order(-summary_df$Max_Difference), ]
print(summary_df)

cat("\n\nInterpretation Guide:\n")
cat("- Large differences (>3 points): Very important factor\n")
cat("- Medium differences (1-3 points): Moderately important factor\n")
cat("- Small differences (<1 point): Less important factor\n")


# Compare practical impact of numeric vs categorical variables
cat("=== PRACTICAL IMPACT COMPARISON ===\n\n")

# For numeric variables: calculate score difference across their range
cat("NUMERIC VARIABLES (Estimated Impact):\n")
cat(strrep("=", 60), "\n\n")

# Attendance (range 60-100, correlation 0.58)
att_range <- max(student_data_filtered$Attendance) - min(student_data_filtered$Attendance)
att_sd <- sd(student_data_filtered$Attendance)
cat("Attendance:\n")
cat("  Correlation: 0.58\n")
cat("  Range: 60-100 (40 points)\n")
cat("  Estimated impact of 10-point increase: ~", round(10 * 0.58, 2), " points\n\n")

# Hours Studied (range 1-44, correlation 0.45)
hrs_range <- max(student_data_filtered$Hours_Studied) - min(student_data_filtered$Hours_Studied)
cat("Hours_Studied:\n")
cat("  Correlation: 0.45\n")
cat("  Range: 1-44 (43 hours)\n")
cat("  Estimated impact of 10-hour increase: ~", round(10 * 0.45, 2), " points\n\n")

# Previous Scores (correlation 0.17)
cat("Previous_Scores:\n")
cat("  Correlation: 0.17\n")
cat("  Range: 50-100 (50 points)\n")
cat("  Estimated impact of 10-point increase: ~", round(10 * 0.17, 2), " points\n\n")

# Tutoring Sessions (correlation 0.16)
cat("Tutoring_Sessions:\n")
cat("  Correlation: 0.16\n")
cat("  Range: 0-8 sessions\n")
cat("  Estimated impact of 1 extra session: ~", round(1 * 0.16, 2), " points\n\n")

cat("\n", strrep("=", 60), "\n")
cat("COMPARISON:\n")
cat("- Attendance impact (10% increase): ~5.8 points\n")
cat("- Hours_Studied impact (10 hours): ~4.5 points\n")
cat("- Best categorical variable (Access_to_Resources): ~1.88 points\n")
cat("\nConclusion: Numeric variables have MUCH larger practical impact!\n")

#WE REMOVE TUTORING SESSIONS -> LOW IMPACT, MOTIVATION_LEVEL, TEACHER_QUALITY, EXTRACURRICULAR_ACTIVITY, DISTANCE_FROM_HOME, FAMILY_INCOME BECAUSE THESE ALL HAVE A LOW IMPACT ON THE AVERAGE SCORE BASED ON DIFFERENT CATEGORIES.  
```

```{r}

# FINAL SIMPLIFIED MODEL - STUDENT EXAM PERFORMANCE

# Based on systematic variable selection:
# - Removed low-variance variables (Internet_Access, Learning_Disabilities, Gender)
# - Removed non-significant variables (Sleep_Hours, Physical_Activity, School_Type)
# - Removed low-impact variables (<1 point difference)
# - Verified no multicollinearity
# 
# FINAL 7 PREDICTORS:
# Numeric (3): Attendance, Hours_Studied, Previous_Scores
# Categorical (4): Access_to_Resources, Parental_Involvement, 
#                  Parental_Education_Level, Peer_Influence


library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(car)


# 1. PREPARE FINAL DATASET


# Select only the variables we need
final_vars <- c("Hours_Studied", "Attendance", "Previous_Scores",
                "Parental_Involvement", "Access_to_Resources", 
                "Peer_Influence", "Parental_Education_Level",
                "Exam_Score")

student_final <- student_data_filtered[, final_vars]

cat("=== FINAL DATASET ===\n")
cat("Variables:", ncol(student_final), "\n")
cat("Observations:", nrow(student_final), "\n")
str(student_final)


# 2. ENCODE CATEGORICAL VARIABLES


# Create ordinal encoding for ordered categories
student_final$Parental_Involvement_num <- 
  as.numeric(factor(student_final$Parental_Involvement, 
                    levels = c("Low", "Medium", "High"), 
                    ordered = TRUE))

student_final$Access_to_Resources_num <- 
  as.numeric(factor(student_final$Access_to_Resources, 
                    levels = c("Low", "Medium", "High"), 
                    ordered = TRUE))

# Convert other categorical to factors for modeling
student_final$Peer_Influence <- 
  factor(student_final$Peer_Influence, 
         levels = c("Negative", "Neutral", "Positive"))

student_final$Parental_Education_Level <- 
  factor(student_final$Parental_Education_Level,
         levels = c("High School", "College", "Postgraduate"))


# 3. BUILD LINEAR REGRESSION MODEL

cat("\n\n=== LINEAR REGRESSION MODEL ===\n")
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n\n")

# Build the model
final_model <- lm(Exam_Score ~ Hours_Studied + Attendance + Previous_Scores +
                    Parental_Involvement_num + Access_to_Resources_num +
                    Peer_Influence + Parental_Education_Level,
                  data = student_final)

# Model summary
summary_model <- summary(final_model)
print(summary_model)

# 4. MODEL DIAGNOSTICS

cat("\n\n=== MODEL DIAGNOSTICS ===\n")
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n\n")

# R-squared
cat("R-squared:", round(summary_model$r.squared, 4), "\n")
cat("Adjusted R-squared:", round(summary_model$adj.r.squared, 4), "\n")
cat("Interpretation:", round(summary_model$adj.r.squared * 100, 1), 
    "% of variance in Exam_Score is explained by the model\n\n")

# RMSE
rmse <- sqrt(mean(final_model$residuals^2))
cat("RMSE (Root Mean Square Error):", round(rmse, 3), "points\n")
cat("Interpretation: Model predictions are typically off by ±", 
    round(rmse, 2), "points\n\n")



# 6. VISUALIZATIONS


cat("\n\n=== GENERATING VISUALIZATIONS ===\n")

# Plot 1: Actual vs Predicted
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))

plot(final_model$fitted.values, student_final$Exam_Score,
     xlab = "Predicted Exam Score", ylab = "Actual Exam Score",
     main = "Actual vs Predicted Exam Scores",
     pch = 16, col = rgb(0, 0, 1, 0.3))
abline(a = 0, b = 1, col = "red", lwd = 2)

# Plot 2: Residuals vs Fitted
plot(final_model$fitted.values, final_model$residuals,
     xlab = "Fitted Values", ylab = "Residuals",
     main = "Residual Plot",
     pch = 16, col = rgb(0, 0, 1, 0.3))
abline(h = 0, col = "red", lwd = 2)

# Plot 3: Q-Q Plot
qqnorm(final_model$residuals, pch = 16, col = rgb(0, 0, 1, 0.3))
qqline(final_model$residuals, col = "red", lwd = 2)

# Plot 4: Scale-Location
plot(final_model$fitted.values, sqrt(abs(final_model$residuals)),
     xlab = "Fitted Values", ylab = "√|Residuals|",
     main = "Scale-Location Plot",
     pch = 16, col = rgb(0, 0, 1, 0.3))

par(mfrow = c(1, 1))


# 7. VARIABLE IMPORTANCE VISUALIZATION


# Calculate standardized coefficients for comparison
# Standardize numeric predictors
student_final_std <- student_final
numeric_predictors <- c("Hours_Studied", "Attendance", "Previous_Scores",
                       "Parental_Involvement_num", "Access_to_Resources_num")

for(var in numeric_predictors) {
  student_final_std[[var]] <- scale(student_final[[var]])
}

# Fit model on standardized data
model_std <- lm(Exam_Score ~ Hours_Studied + Attendance + Previous_Scores +
                  Parental_Involvement_num + Access_to_Resources_num +
                  Peer_Influence + Parental_Education_Level,
                data = student_final_std)

# Extract standardized coefficients
std_coefs <- coef(model_std)[-1]  # Remove intercept
std_coefs_numeric <- std_coefs[1:5]  # Only numeric for fair comparison


# 8. SUMMARY REPORT


cat("\n\n")
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")
cat("                    FINAL MODEL SUMMARY                    \n")
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n\n")

cat("MODEL PERFORMANCE:\n")
cat("- Adjusted R²:", round(summary_model$adj.r.squared, 4), "\n")
cat("- RMSE:", round(rmse, 3), "points\n")
cat("- Variables: 7 predictors\n")
cat("- Observations:", nrow(student_final), "\n\n")



cat("\nKEY INSIGHTS:\n")
cat("1. Attendance has the strongest impact on exam scores\n")
cat("2. Hours studied and access to resources also play major roles\n")
cat("3. All 7 variables are statistically significant\n")
cat("4. Model explains ~", round(summary_model$adj.r.squared * 100, 1), 
    "% of variance in exam performance\n")

cat("\n", strrep("=", 70), "\n")


```

```{r}

# DIAGNOSTIC ANALYSIS & FIXES


# 1. Check for outliers
cat("=== CHECKING FOR OUTLIERS ===\n")
student_final$residuals <- final_model$residuals
student_final$fitted <- final_model$fitted.values

# Identify extreme outliers (residuals > 3 SD)
outlier_threshold <- 3 * sd(student_final$residuals)
outliers <- student_final[abs(student_final$residuals) > outlier_threshold, ]

cat("Number of outliers (>3 SD):", nrow(outliers), "\n")
cat("Percentage of data:", round(nrow(outliers)/nrow(student_final)*100, 2), "%\n\n")


# 2. Check distribution of Exam_Score
cat("\n=== EXAM SCORE DISTRIBUTION ===\n")
hist(student_final$Exam_Score, breaks = 30, 
     main = "Distribution of Exam Scores",
     xlab = "Exam Score", col = "lightblue")
abline(v = mean(student_final$Exam_Score), col = "red", lwd = 2)

# Manual skewness calculation
mean_score <- mean(student_final$Exam_Score)
sd_score <- sd(student_final$Exam_Score)
n <- length(student_final$Exam_Score)
skewness <- (sum((student_final$Exam_Score - mean_score)^3) / n) / (sd_score^3)

cat("\nSkewness:", round(skewness, 3), "\n")
cat("(0 = symmetric, >0 = right-skewed, <0 = left-skewed)\n")




cat("\nExam Score Range:\n")
cat("Min:", min(student_final$Exam_Score), "\n")
cat("Max:", max(student_final$Exam_Score), "\n")
cat("Mean:", round(mean(student_final$Exam_Score), 2), "\n")
cat("Median:", median(student_final$Exam_Score), "\n")

# 5. Summary of issues
cat("\n=== SUMMARY OF DIAGNOSTIC FINDINGS ===\n")
cat("1. Outliers:", nrow(outliers), "observations (", 
    round(nrow(outliers)/nrow(student_final)*100, 2), "%)\n")
#cat("2. Impossible values (>100):", nrow(extreme_high), "\n")
cat("3. Skewness:", round(skewness, 3), 
    ifelse(abs(skewness) > 1, " (HIGH - distribution is skewed)", 
           " (acceptable)\n"))
```

```{r}

# FIX THE MODEL


cat("=== CLEANING DATA ===\n")

# Step 1: Remove impossible values (>100)
student_cleaned <- student_final[student_final$Exam_Score <= 100, ]
cat("Removed scores >100:", nrow(student_final) - nrow(student_cleaned), "\n")

# Step 2: Remove extreme outliers (optional - let's see with and without)
# Calculate outliers on cleaned data
model_temp <- lm(Exam_Score ~ Hours_Studied + Attendance + Previous_Scores +
                   Parental_Involvement_num + Access_to_Resources_num +
                   Peer_Influence + Parental_Education_Level,
                 data = student_cleaned)

student_cleaned$residuals_temp <- model_temp$residuals
outlier_threshold <- 3 * sd(student_cleaned$residuals_temp)

# Option A: Keep outliers (more conservative)
student_final_v2 <- student_cleaned

# Option B: Remove outliers (cleaner model)
student_final_v3 <- student_cleaned[abs(student_cleaned$residuals_temp) <= outlier_threshold, ]

cat("Original data:", nrow(student_final), "\n")
cat("After removing >100:", nrow(student_final_v2), "\n")
cat("After removing outliers too:", nrow(student_final_v3), "\n\n")


# REFIT MODEL - VERSION 1 (Remove only >100)

cat("=== MODEL V2: Removed Impossible Values Only ===\n")
model_v2 <- lm(Exam_Score ~ Hours_Studied + Attendance + Previous_Scores +
                 Parental_Involvement_num + Access_to_Resources_num +
                 Peer_Influence + Parental_Education_Level,
               data = student_final_v2)

summary_v2 <- summary(model_v2)
print(summary_v2)

cat("\nR-squared:", round(summary_v2$r.squared, 4), "\n")
cat("Adjusted R-squared:", round(summary_v2$adj.r.squared, 4), "\n")
cat("RMSE:", round(sqrt(mean(model_v2$residuals^2)), 3), "\n")

# REFIT MODEL - VERSION 2 (Remove outliers too)


cat("\n\n=== MODEL V3: Removed Impossible Values + Outliers ===\n")
model_v3 <- lm(Exam_Score ~ Hours_Studied + Attendance + Previous_Scores +
                 Parental_Involvement_num + Access_to_Resources_num +
                 Peer_Influence + Parental_Education_Level,
               data = student_final_v3)

summary_v3 <- summary(model_v3)
print(summary_v3)

cat("\nR-squared:", round(summary_v3$r.squared, 4), "\n")
cat("Adjusted R-squared:", round(summary_v3$adj.r.squared, 4), "\n")
cat("RMSE:", round(sqrt(mean(model_v3$residuals^2)), 3), "\n")

# Check diagnostics
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(model_v3)
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

# COMPARISON

cat("\n\n=== MODEL COMPARISON ===\n")
cat(sprintf("%-30s %10s %10s %10s\n", "Metric", "Original", "V2 (>100)", "V3 (+outliers)"))
cat(strrep("-", 65), "\n")
cat(sprintf("%-30s %10d %10d %10d\n", "N observations", 
            nrow(student_final), nrow(student_final_v2), nrow(student_final_v3)))
cat(sprintf("%-30s %10.4f %10.4f %10.4f\n", "Adj R-squared", 
            summary(final_model)$adj.r.squared, summary_v2$adj.r.squared, summary_v3$adj.r.squared))
cat(sprintf("%-30s %10.3f %10.3f %10.3f\n", "RMSE", 
            sqrt(mean(final_model$residuals^2)), 
            sqrt(mean(model_v2$residuals^2)),
            sqrt(mean(model_v3$residuals^2))))


```
